summary: The agent analyzed the codebase and produced an ANALYSIS.md report covering the tech stack, dependencies, input component details, usage mapping, file dependency graph, and plugin integration points. Key finding: this is a terminal CLI application using Ink (React for terminals) with `ink-text-input`, not a web frontend. The primary input component is `PromptInput` at `src/cli/ui/PromptInput.tsx`, a minimal ~50-line wrapper, with behavioral complexity residing in the parent `App` component's state machine.
decisions: Identified the application as a terminal CLI app using Ink (React for terminals) rather than a web frontend
Identified `ink-text-input` as the sole input library and `PromptInput` (src/cli/ui/PromptInput.tsx) as the primary input component
Recognized that behavioral complexity (mode switching, input queuing, slash commands) lives in the parent App component, not in the input wrapper itself
Organized the report into six sections: tech stack, dependencies, input component deep-dive, usage mapping with data flow diagrams, file dependency graph, and key observations
Noted that the input serves triple duty: starting workflows, answering pipeline questions, and accepting slash commands

concerns.description: The agent created ANALYSIS.md in the project root, which is a documentation artifact that may not belong in the repository long-term (should likely be in a docs/ folder or removed after consumption by downstream tasks)

The agent's response summary mentions the report content but we cannot verify the completeness or accuracy of the ANALYSIS.md file contents since only the summary was provided in the output, not the full file

The finding that this is a terminal CLI app (not a web frontend) is significant context that may fundamentally change how downstream tasks (e.g., adding rich input features or plugins) should be approached

reviewFindings.description: The application is a terminal CLI using Ink (React for terminals), not a web frontend. This fundamentally constrains plugin/component choices — web-oriented UI libraries won't work. Any downstream tasks expecting a web stack need to be re-scoped.

PromptInput is a minimal ~50-line wrapper around ink-text-input that adds a prompt character, loading spinner, and trim-on-submit. Feature enhancements would likely need to either extend this wrapper or replace the underlying ink-text-input dependency.

All behavioral complexity (mode switching, input queuing, slash commands, pipeline interaction) resides in the parent App component's state machine, not in the input component itself. Changes to input behavior may require modifying App rather than PromptInput.
The ANALYSIS.md report was created in the project root. Consider moving it to a docs/ directory or treating it as a transient artifact for downstream task consumption only.
